competencies.description: Recognise right triangles embedded in real scenes.
Identify opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse from a chosen acute angle.
Define sin, cos, and tan using triangle sides.
Relate cosec, sec, and cot to reciprocal forms.
Connect tan and cot to sin and cos.
Understand that trigonometric ratios remain constant for the same angle.
Recover missing sides and ratios from one known ratio in a right triangle.
Derive trigonometric values for 45 degrees.
Derive trigonometric values for 30 degrees and 60 degrees.
Understand 0 degree and 90 degree values and undefined ratios.
Recall the standard value table from 0 to 90 degrees.
Use the standard values in mixed calculations and angle recovery.
Derive sin^2 A + cos^2 A = 1.
Derive sec^2 A - tan^2 A = 1.
Derive cosec^2 A = 1 + cot^2 A.
Express one trigonometric ratio in terms of another.
Complete multi-step identity proofs modeled on NCERT.
Use line of sight, angle of elevation, and angle of depression correctly.
Solve tower, ladder, and direct elevation problems.
Account for observer height or pedestal height in applications.
Solve shadow, equal-pole, and canal-width patterns.
Handle lighthouse, balloon, and moving-car style applications.
Combine multiple ratios and scene constraints in a capstone mission.

revision_pool.prompt: Swap the reference angle and re-label the sides.
Match each ratio to its reciprocal partner.
Tap the correct standard value before the timer expires.
Repair the broken identity in two valid moves.
Re-aim the line of sight and confirm the correct angle type.

worlds.title: World 1 - Triangle Lab
worlds.learning_objective: Build the language of trigonometry from right triangles and ratio meaning.

mastery_gate.encouragement_copy: You are building the foundations. Retry, refine, and lock in the angle-to-side story.

worlds.title: World 2 - Sacred Angles
worlds.learning_objective: Derive, remember, and deploy the standard values from 0 to 90 degrees.

mastery_gate.encouragement_copy: Standard values should feel familiar, not frightening. You can always practice and come back stronger.

worlds.title: World 3 - Identity Forge
worlds.learning_objective: Turn triangle facts into identities and learn to transform expressions with confidence.

mastery_gate.encouragement_copy: Each proof is a sequence of valid moves. Slow thinking is good thinking here.

worlds.title: World 4 - Height Seeker
worlds.learning_objective: Apply trigonometry to heights, distances, motion, and multi-step real scenes.

mastery_gate.encouragement_copy: You are now using trigonometry the way the chapter intended: to understand the world around you.

levels.title: Hidden Triangles
levels.story_setup: Students scan landmark scenes and lock onto the right triangle hidden in each setup.
levels.objective: Spot the horizontal, vertical, and line-of-sight edges that create a right triangle.
hint_setup: Look for the 90-degree corner first.
The line of sight is the slanted edge.

learning_loop.build: Drag the angle finder over each scene until the right-angle corner glows.
learning_loop.recall: Quick sort scenes into right triangle or not-right-triangle.
learning_loop.apply: Confirm the triangle hidden in Qutub Minar, riverbank, and balloon scenes.
learning_loop.revise: Wrong scenes return later with lighter overlays.
learning_loop.master: Complete three scene detections with at least two accurate first tries.

levels.title: Angle Side Lab
levels.story_setup: The lab triangle changes reference angle and asks the student to relabel every side.
levels.objective: Assign opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse correctly as the reference angle changes.
hint_setup: Opposite is the side facing the chosen angle.
Hypotenuse always stays opposite the right angle.

learning_loop.build: Drag side labels to the correct triangle edges for angle A and angle C.
learning_loop.recall: Flash relabel two mini-triangles with swapped reference angles.
learning_loop.apply: Use the labels to explain why the same side changes role when the angle changes.
learning_loop.revise: The game replays the incorrect side assignment with highlights.
learning_loop.master: Correctly relabel both reference-angle views in one run.

levels.title: Ratio Link Forge
levels.story_setup: The forge accepts only correct numerator-denominator pairings.
levels.objective: Build sin, cos, tan, and cot from the right side relationships.
hint_setup: Ask which sides touch the reference angle.
Tan compares opposite to adjacent.

learning_loop.build: Drag side tiles into numerator and denominator slots.
learning_loop.recall: Rapidly complete partial ratio cards before time runs out.
learning_loop.apply: Use the built ratios to explain the same triangle from two angles.
learning_loop.revise: Broken ratio cards return until the correct build is stable.
learning_loop.master: Forge all requested ratios without using the final safety hint.

levels.title: Reciprocal Switchboard
levels.story_setup: A switchboard scrambles direct and reciprocal ratios until the player repairs the links.
levels.objective: Connect sin with cosec, cos with sec, and tan with cot using reciprocal logic.
hint_setup: A reciprocal flips numerator and denominator.
Only true reciprocal pairs complete the circuit.

learning_loop.build: Reconnect scrambled direct and reciprocal ratio circuits.
learning_loop.recall: Flash identify which reciprocal belongs to the active ratio.
learning_loop.apply: Repair a side-ratio machine by replacing each ratio with its reciprocal.
learning_loop.revise: Miswired pairs reappear as short drills later in the world.
learning_loop.master: Repair all reciprocal circuits and explain one match in words.

levels.title: Same Angle Signal
levels.story_setup: A beam passes through triangles of different sizes but the same acute angle.
levels.objective: Show that triangles with the same acute angle keep the same trigonometric ratios.
hint_setup: Watch the angle stay fixed while the size changes.
The ratio should stay stable even as sides scale.

learning_loop.build: Stretch and shrink similar triangles while measuring the same angle.
learning_loop.recall: Predict whether the ratio changes before the scale animation runs.
learning_loop.apply: Lock onto matching ratio readings across three triangle sizes.
learning_loop.revise: Incorrect predictions come back with side-length overlays.
learning_loop.master: Confirm constant ratio readings across all scale variants.

levels.title: Triangle Lab Mastery
levels.story_setup: The lab locks until one ratio is used to reconstruct the rest of the triangle story.
levels.objective: Recover missing ratios or sides from one known ratio and a right-triangle relation.
hint_setup: Convert the known ratio into side relationships first.
Use the Pythagoras link only after the side ratio is fixed.

learning_loop.build: Start from one known ratio and rebuild the other ratios in sequence.
learning_loop.recall: Spot the direct consequence of a given tan or sec value.
learning_loop.apply: Solve mixed NCERT-style ratio recovery cards.
learning_loop.revise: Missed ratio chains re-enter the revision queue.
learning_loop.master: Clear the mixed ratio gauntlet with world-pass accuracy.

rounds.prompt: Given tan A = 4/3, build sin A
Given sin A = 1/3, choose the side relation before computing anything else
Given sec A = 13/12, build cos A

levels.title: Forty-Five Crystal
levels.story_setup: A crystal only opens when the player builds an isosceles right triangle and extracts the 45-degree values.
levels.objective: Construct the 45-45-90 triangle and derive sin 45, cos 45, and tan 45.
hint_setup: In a 45-45-90 triangle, the legs match.
The hypotenuse grows by root 2.

learning_loop.build: Drag the triangle until the two legs balance.
learning_loop.recall: Tap the correct 45-degree values before the crystal fades.
learning_loop.apply: Use the built triangle to verify the three core ratios.
learning_loop.revise: Wrong ratios return as one-card flash repairs.
learning_loop.master: Derive all three 45-degree values in one complete run.

levels.title: Thirty Sixty Anvil
levels.story_setup: An equilateral triangle is split on the anvil to reveal the 30-degree and 60-degree patterns.
levels.objective: Derive the 30-degree and 60-degree ratios from an equilateral split.
hint_setup: Split the equilateral triangle through its midpoint.
The short half-base becomes the side opposite 30 degrees.

learning_loop.build: Split the equilateral triangle and verify the 30-60-90 structure.
learning_loop.recall: Match each angle to its exact ratio value.
learning_loop.apply: Use the split triangle to confirm sin, cos, and tan for both angles.
learning_loop.revise: Missed value cards return in reversed angle order.
learning_loop.master: Complete the full 30-60 derivation without a formula sheet.

levels.title: Edge Angle Observatory
levels.story_setup: An observatory slider moves the acute angle down to 0 degrees and up to 90 degrees.
levels.objective: Understand 0 degree and 90 degree values, and identify which ratios become undefined.
hint_setup: Watch what happens to the side opposite the angle as the ray flattens.
Undefined means the denominator collapses to zero.

learning_loop.build: Slide the ray to 0 degrees and 90 degrees and observe the changing side lengths.
learning_loop.recall: Tag each ratio as defined or undefined at the edge angles.
learning_loop.apply: Use the observatory to justify sin 0, cos 0, sin 90, and cos 90.
learning_loop.revise: Undefined-ratio errors return as short edge cases.
learning_loop.master: Explain both edge-angle states and classify all six ratios correctly.

levels.title: Table Memory Arena
levels.story_setup: The arena rewards fast, confident recall of the standard value table.
levels.objective: Recall the values of sin, cos, tan, cosec, sec, and cot at 0, 30, 45, 60, and 90 degrees.
hint_setup: Remember the table pattern, not isolated facts.
Undefined values appear where the denominator becomes zero.

learning_loop.build: Assemble the angle table row by row before the timer starts.
learning_loop.recall: Tap the right value tile for each prompt in a timed streak.
learning_loop.apply: Recover any broken row by memory without deriving it again.
learning_loop.revise: Missed prompts return later with only two distractors.
learning_loop.master: Hit the target streak and accuracy together.

levels.title: Sacred Angles Mastery
levels.story_setup: The final shrine mixes standard values, complementary reasoning, and NCERT-style angle recovery.
levels.objective: Apply the standard values in mixed expressions and angle recovery patterns from Exercise 8.2.
hint_setup: Simplify one ratio at a time.
Recover the angle by matching the exact ratio value.

learning_loop.build: Warm up by rebuilding selected table entries from memory.
learning_loop.recall: Solve standard-value prompts in a mixed order.
learning_loop.apply: Answer NCERT-style expression and angle-recovery rounds.
learning_loop.revise: Any weak angle returns to the arena queue.
learning_loop.master: Pass the mixed sacred-angle gauntlet.

rounds.prompt: If sin(A - B) = 1/2 and A > B, choose A - B

levels.title: Pythagorean Core
levels.story_setup: The forge extracts the first identity straight from a right triangle.
levels.objective: Derive sin^2 A + cos^2 A = 1 by normalising the Pythagoras relation.
hint_setup: Start from AB^2 + BC^2 = AC^2.
Divide every term by the same squared side.

learning_loop.build: Drag the division move onto each term of the triangle equation.
learning_loop.recall: Identify which side to divide by to produce sin and cos terms.
learning_loop.apply: Complete the final identity chain in the correct order.
learning_loop.revise: Incorrect derivation steps return with only the valid move set.
learning_loop.master: Derive the identity without revealing the final line.

moves.label: Replace with cos^2 A and sin^2 A
Replace with sec^2 A and tan^2 A

levels.title: Sec Tan Reactor
levels.story_setup: The reactor converts the triangle relation into a sec and tan identity.
levels.objective: Derive sec^2 A - tan^2 A = 1 using the same right triangle core.
hint_setup: Use the triangle equation again.
This time divide by AB^2 and simplify.

learning_loop.build: Route the triangle equation through the AB^2 reactor path.
learning_loop.recall: Choose which quotient becomes tan^2 A and which becomes sec^2 A.
learning_loop.apply: Finish the identity in the simplified order demanded by NCERT.
learning_loop.revise: Wrong move sequences return as two-step repairs.
learning_loop.master: Stabilise the sec-tan reactor without the emergency hint.

moves.label: Rename to 1 + tan^2 A = sec^2 A
Rename to cot^2 A + 1 = cosec^2 A

levels.title: Cosec Cot Reactor
levels.story_setup: The third forge channel turns the triangle relation into the cosec-cot identity.
levels.objective: Derive cosec^2 A = 1 + cot^2 A.
hint_setup: Divide the triangle equation by BC^2.
Track which quotient becomes cot A.

learning_loop.build: Send the expression through the BC^2 channel.
learning_loop.recall: Match each quotient to cot or cosec.
learning_loop.apply: Finish the identity in NCERT form.
learning_loop.revise: The wrong quotient mapping returns later as a mini-fix round.
learning_loop.master: Complete the reactor path in one steady sequence.

moves.label: Rename to cot^2 A + 1 = cosec^2 A
Rename to tan^2 A + 1 = sec^2 A

levels.title: Ratio Expression Converter
levels.story_setup: The converter rewrites ratios in terms of one known ratio without losing validity.
levels.objective: Express ratios such as cos A, tan A, and sec A in terms of sin A or cot A.
hint_setup: Start from the identity that actually contains the target ratio.
Use the positive square-root branch because the angle is acute.

learning_loop.build: Choose a valid identity and isolate the target ratio.
learning_loop.recall: Spot which identity is usable for the requested rewrite.
learning_loop.apply: Complete NCERT-style expression conversion rounds.
learning_loop.revise: Any broken derivation returns as a smaller isolate-the-ratio task.
learning_loop.master: Rewrite all requested ratios correctly.

rounds.expression: Express cos A in terms of sin A
Express tan A in terms of sin A

moves.label: Use tan A = sin A / cos A
Use tan A = cos A / sin A

levels.title: Identity Forge Mastery
levels.story_setup: The final forge mixes derivation, rewriting, and proof completion like Exercise 8.3.
levels.objective: Complete multi-step proof and simplification tasks without seeing the full solution path.
hint_setup: Each step must be valid on its own.
If stuck, rewrite in sin and cos first.

learning_loop.build: Reconstruct the proof flow from shuffled valid moves.
learning_loop.recall: Recognise the quickest valid first step.
learning_loop.apply: Clear mixed identities modeled on Exercise 8.3.
learning_loop.revise: Weak proof patterns enter the revision queue.
learning_loop.master: Pass the forge gauntlet with world-pass accuracy.

moves.label: Rewrite in sin A and cos A
Rewrite sec and tan over cos

levels.title: Line of Sight Drill
levels.story_setup: A scout lens trains the player to classify angle of elevation and angle of depression correctly.
levels.objective: Use the line of sight and horizontal correctly in elevation and depression scenes.
hint_setup: Elevation means the line of sight is above the horizontal.
Depression means the line of sight drops below the horizontal.

learning_loop.build: Aim the sight line and place the horizontal reference in each scene.
learning_loop.recall: Classify the angle type in short fast rounds.
learning_loop.apply: Resolve balcony, tower, and observer scenes with correct line placement.
learning_loop.revise: Misclassified scenes return with a partial horizontal guide.
learning_loop.master: Clear all angle-type and line-of-sight checks.

scenarios.title: Balcony to flower pot
scenarios.question: Angle of elevation or depression?
scenarios.hint_equation: Use the horizontal from the observer first

scenarios.title: Lighthouse to ship
scenarios.question: Angle of elevation or depression?
scenarios.hint_equation: The ship is below the observer

levels.title: Tower and Ladder Relay
levels.story_setup: The relay alternates tower height and ladder length rescue tasks.
levels.objective: Solve direct elevation cases such as tower height, rope height, and ladder placement.
hint_setup: Choose the ratio linking the known side and the missing side.
Use the ladder as the hypotenuse when it leans.

learning_loop.build: Adjust angle and length tools until the scene geometry matches the prompt.
learning_loop.recall: Quickly choose the correct ratio family for each mission.
learning_loop.apply: Solve tower, rope, ladder, and kite-style missions.
learning_loop.revise: The same mission reappears later with gentler numbers.
learning_loop.master: Complete the relay without revealing final answers.

scenarios.title: Tower 15 m away
scenarios.question: Find the tower height when angle = 60

scenarios.title: Ladder at 60
scenarios.question: Find the ladder length when vertical reach is 3.7

scenarios.title: Kite string
scenarios.question: Find string length when height is 60 and angle = 60

levels.title: Observer Height Watch
levels.story_setup: This watchtower mission adds observer height, pedestal height, and flagstaff layers.
levels.objective: Account for extra height components before solving the trigonometric step.
hint_setup: Separate the geometric height from the observer or pedestal offset first.
The trig part often solves only part of the full height.

learning_loop.build: Stack object heights into the scene before measuring.
learning_loop.recall: Identify which height is already included and which is extra.
learning_loop.apply: Solve observer-height and flagstaff-on-building cases.
learning_loop.revise: Missed stack-ups return as short offset-only drills.
learning_loop.master: Resolve all offset cases correctly.

scenarios.title: Observer and chimney
scenarios.question: Find total chimney height from a 1.5 m observer at 45

scenarios.title: Building and flagstaff
scenarios.question: Find flagstaff height when building is 10 and top angle changes from 30 to 45

scenarios.title: Statue on pedestal
scenarios.question: Find pedestal height when the statue is 1.6 high
scenarios.hint_equation: Subtract the statue height after solving the total

levels.title: Shadow Canal Grid
levels.story_setup: The grid combines shadows, equal poles, and canal-width patterns in one tactical map.
levels.objective: Solve two-angle and shared-height patterns from Exercise 9.1.
hint_setup: When the same height appears twice, set the two expressions equal.
Use the wider shadow or wider road relation carefully.

learning_loop.build: Lay down distances and compare the paired triangles on the grid.
learning_loop.recall: Choose whether to subtract, equate, or add the two expressions.
learning_loop.apply: Solve shadow, equal-pole, and canal-width tasks.
learning_loop.revise: Weak pattern types re-enter the grid as one-step versions.
learning_loop.master: Clear the full paired-triangle grid.

scenarios.title: Shadow difference
scenarios.question: Find tower height when the shadow differs by 40 between 30 and 60
scenarios.hint_equation: Use tan 60 = h/x and tan 30 = h/(x+40)

scenarios.title: Equal poles across road
scenarios.question: Find the common height and distances on an 80 m road
scenarios.hint_equation: Set the two height expressions equal

scenarios.title: Canal width
scenarios.question: Find the canal width when the second observation point is 20 m farther
scenarios.hint_equation: Use tan 60 and tan 30 with a shared tower height

levels.title: Lighthouse Moving Targets
levels.story_setup: Ships, balloons, and cars move while the player tracks angle changes over time.
levels.objective: Solve moving-object and angle-of-depression patterns from Exercise 9.1.
hint_setup: Translate each angle into a right triangle before thinking about motion.
Compare positions at the two observation moments.

learning_loop.build: Track the motion path and pin the two observation triangles.
learning_loop.recall: Identify whether the answer is a difference, a distance traveled, or a time remaining.
learning_loop.apply: Solve lighthouse, balloon, and highway-car patterns.
learning_loop.revise: Missed moving scenes return with one triangle already drawn.
learning_loop.master: Resolve all changing-angle scenarios.

scenarios.title: Lighthouse ships
scenarios.question: Find the distance between two ships seen at depression angles 30 and 45
scenarios.hint_equation: Use the same lighthouse height for both ships

scenarios.title: Balloon drift
scenarios.question: Find the horizontal distance traveled as the angle changes from 60 to 30

scenarios.title: Highway car
scenarios.question: Track the car from 30 to 60 depression and infer the remaining time
scenarios.hint_equation: The second triangle is closer but shares the same tower height

levels.title: Capstone Rescue Network
levels.story_setup: A rescue network spans towers, canals, moving scouts, and layered sightlines in one final operation.
levels.objective: Combine multiple Chapter 8 and Chapter 9 competencies in a multi-step capstone mission.
hint_setup: Break the capstone into smaller right triangles.
Use only the equation setup needed for the current step.

learning_loop.build: Map the full rescue scene into linked right triangles.
learning_loop.recall: Select the correct ratio family for each stage.
learning_loop.apply: Finish the full multi-step mission using angle, ratio, and application logic together.
learning_loop.revise: Any weak stage is broken into a lighter replay on the next attempt.
learning_loop.master: Pass the final rescue with chapter-level mastery.

scenarios.title: Relay rescue
scenarios.question: Use a tower height, canal width, and moving scout angle change to route the rescue line.
scenarios.hint_equation: Step 1: tan A = height / distance. Step 2: compare the shared height expressions.
